marketState: neutral - bullish weather/positioning skew for the medium term, bearish short-term technical momentum
summary: Price ($2.846 front) is trading roughly in line with modeled fair value at every horizon (spreads -$0.08 to -$0.16, all flagged FAIR to SLIGHTLY UNDER), so there is no large valuation edge in either direction. The standout fundamental signal is weather: demand is running +6.1% vs 5y over the next week and +27.1% vs 5y (rangePos 0.96, near the top of the 5y range) over days 8-16, pointing to unusually hot, high-demand conditions building into peak cooling season. That is offset by a bearish storage picture (+180 Bcf vs 5y surplus, and a +22 Bcf bearish build surprise) and by seasonality that sits 9.6pp below its typical-year path with weak (38%) odds of being higher 3-4 weeks out, though seasonal reliability is explicitly flagged Low this week. Managed money is net short at just the 8th percentile of the last 5 years, an extreme positioning skew that historically raises short-covering risk over a multi-week horizon. Near-term, however, all six technical timeframes (5m-1w) are bearish with 50<200 EMA crosses and the curve shows a small front-month backwardation, arguing for caution/downside over the next 1-10 days even though the medium-term fundamental mix leans constructive.
